steps.title: Clear the denominators
steps.content:
Start with the given equation.
$$\frac{x+7}{4}=\frac{x+7}{16}$$
Multiply both sides by $16$.
$$4(x+7)=x+7$$

steps.title: Find the target value
steps.content:
Subtract $x+7$ from both sides.
$$3(x+7)=0$$
$$x+7=0$$
The value $0$ is between $-3$ and $3$. The answer is B.
